note.text: Adult-onset asthma, moderate persistent. Environmental trigger (cat dander).

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4-6 hours as needed for wheeze/shortness of breath

coding.display: budesonide 0.09 MG/ACTUAT Dry Powder Inhaler [Pulmicort Flexhaler]

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth twice daily

resource.conclusion: Spirometry shows mild airflow obstruction consistent with moderate persistent asthma. FEV1 3.05L (77% predicted), FEV1/FVC 77%. ACT 20 - well controlled on current regimen.

text.div: Patient reports asthma reasonably controlled. Rescue inhaler used about once weekly, mostly with exercise. No recent ED visits. Never smoker.
VS: BP 122/78, SpO2 97% RA. Lungs clear, mild expiratory wheeze on forced exhalation. Spirometry: FEV1 3.05L, FVC 3.95L, FEV1/FVC 77%. PEF 365 L/min. ACT score 20.
Asthma, moderate persistent, reasonably controlled on ICS monotherapy.
Continue budesonide (Pulmicort) 90mcg 2 puffs BID and albuterol PRN. Reviewed inhaler technique and cat dander avoidance. Routine follow-up in 6 months.

resource.conclusion: Spirometry essentially stable versus prior. FEV1 3.0L, FEV1/FVC 77%. ACT 19 - reasonably controlled.

text.div: Reports stable symptoms, similar rescue inhaler frequency to last visit. No ED visits since last seen.
VS: BP 124/79, SpO2 97% RA. Lungs clear, trace expiratory wheeze. Spirometry: FEV1 3.0L, FVC 3.9L, FEV1/FVC 77%. PEF 355 L/min. ACT score 19.
Continue current regimen. Routine follow-up in 6 months.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime

resource.conclusion: Mild decline versus prior visits. FEV1 2.9L (down from 3.05L), FEV1/FVC 75%. ACT 17 - control worsening. Added montelukast for allergic component; recommend follow-up in 3 months to reassess.

text.div: Reports slightly more frequent rescue inhaler use over the past month, mostly seasonal (fall allergens). No ED visits.
VS: BP 126/80, SpO2 96% RA. Lungs clear, mild expiratory wheeze. Spirometry: FEV1 2.9L, FVC 3.85L, FEV1/FVC 75% (mildly reduced from prior). PEF 340 L/min. ACT score 17 (down from 19).
Asthma, moderate persistent, mild worsening of control - likely allergic component given seasonal pattern.
Add montelukast 10mg nightly. Continue budesonide and albuterol PRN. Follow-up in 3 months to reassess control; sooner if symptoms worsen.
